extra_model_fields.post_revision_expected_outcome: Verifier should be able to confirm presence of formulas and check cells even if openpyxl is unavailable, and rubric item ri_1 should pass after re-evaluation.
success_criteria_for_revision: A formulas.txt file present that enables auditors to confirm formulas without parsing the .xlsx
Acceptance Checks sheet present with PASS/FAIL driven by formulas and visible row-level citations
CSV exports of raw packet and acceptance checks included
README clarifies verification path and which score is authoritative

extra_model_fields.post_revision_expected_outcome: Verifier should be able to confirm presence of formulas and check cells even if openpyxl is unavailable, and rubric item ri_1 should pass after re-evaluation.
success_criteria_for_revision: A formulas.txt file present that enables auditors to confirm formulas without parsing the .xlsx
Acceptance Checks sheet present with PASS/FAIL driven by formulas and visible row-level citations
CSV exports of raw packet and acceptance checks included
README clarifies verification path and which score is authoritative

planned_changes.description: Add a formula audit export (formulas.txt) listing every computed cell used for acceptance checks and remediation calculations: sheet name, cell address, exact formula, and short comment linking to the acceptance criterion.

Add an 'Acceptance Checks' sheet that contains PASS/FAIL cells implemented with formulas (no hard-coded PASS/FAIL). Each PASS/FAIL cell must reference explicit input cells and formulas and include a trailing comment cell pointing to the formula address used for the decision.
Include CSV export(s) of the raw packet (safety_PLC_2_raw_packet.csv) and of the computed acceptance checks (safety_PLC_2_acceptance_checks.csv). These enable verification without openpyxl.

Where the memo or workbook reports derived numbers (e.g., remediation costs after percent change, budget comparisons), ensure those cells contain formulas. Document any originally hard-coded numbers in the formula audit with an explicit explanation; replace with formulas where appropriate.

Add README.md in the attempt artifacts describing: (a) which score path should be used for pass/fail decisions (deterministic vs model), (b) the files included for audit (formulas.txt, CSVs, optional screenshots), and (c) guidance for re-evaluators on how to verify acceptance checks quickly.

If the grader output is produced by your systems, ensure failed_criteria/passed_criteria and rubric_item_scores are consistent before submission. If multiple graders run, label their outputs (deterministic_passed, model_passed).

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Workbook internals were unparseable in the review environment and grader metadata contained inconsistent pass/fail flags. Provide auditable evidence of formulas, row-level citations, and acceptance checks; and normalize grading metadata.

README clarifies verification path and which score is authoritative

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Workbook internals were unparseable in the review environment and grader metadata contained inconsistent pass/fail flags. Provide auditable evidence of formulas, row-level citations, and acceptance checks; and normalize grading metadata.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Required artifacts are present and the expert memo documents the packet, remediation options, budget, and correctly preserves ambiguity (recommendation: needs_review). However the grader could not parse the workbook in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so the existence of in-workbook formulas, row-level citations, and machine-checkable acceptance checks could not be independently verified. The verifier also flagged internal inconsistencies in the grader metadata (conflicting passed/failed flags). To achieve full credit, provide auditable evidence that workbook checks are implemented as described and resolve metadata inconsistencies.
